title: 2 of the best boat tours in America are in Maine, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Acadian Boat Tours in Bar Harbor, Maine, ranked No. 8 in USA Today's 2026 10Best Readers' Choice Awards.
Sea Princess Cruises in Northeast Harbor, Maine, secured the No. 10 position in the boat tours category.
The top boat tour in the U.S., according to the awards, is the Chicago Architecture Center River Cruise.

title: As the World Cup comes to Foxborough, homeowners are renting out their lawns for parking
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Residents near Gillette Stadium are offering their yards for World Cup parking, charging $100 per spot.
Foxborough prohibits residential parking lots due to a 2012 bylaw, but Walpole issues permits through the Select Board.
5,000 stadium-controlled and 6,000 satellite parking spots are available, per FIFA World Cup Boston officials.

title: Hopkinton police seeking information after ‘suspicious video’ of person walking into roadway at night circulates online
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Hopkinton police are investigating a video showing a person in a construction vest and mask walking onto a road at night.
The video was posted on Reddit, depicting an individual with a shovel and Halloween mask crossing a road as a car swerves.
Police urge anyone with information about the incident to contact Hopkinton authorities at 508-497-3401.

title: Boston City Council approves $1.7 billion schools budget that cuts hundreds of jobs
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council approved a $1.73 billion budget for Boston Public Schools, eliminating over 400 jobs.
Boston Public Schools budget cuts are driven by declining enrollment and rising health care costs, impacting mostly paraprofessionals.
Boston Teachers Union opposed the budget, urging for state aid and alternative funding to prevent job cuts.

title: Boozy ice cream is coming to Boston
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Tipsy Scoop plans to open a boozy ice cream store at 59 Dartmouth St. in Boston's South End next spring.
The New York-based company will offer catering and delivery of their alcohol-infused ice creams before the store opens.
Owner Kimberly Medina will manage the Boston franchise, highlighting its personal significance due to her family ties to the area.

title: Kevin McHale says Larry Bird would light up the modern NBA like a ‘five-alarm fire’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kevin McHale claims Larry Bird would excel in today's NBA, outshining players like Luka Doncic.
Bird's career highlights include three NBA titles, three MVP awards, and shooting .376 from 3-point range.
McHale cites Bird's durability, shooting, and defense, saying advances in sports science would enhance his modern impact.

title: Boston Police Officer Nicholas O’Malley pleads not guilty to manslaughter in deadly Roxbury shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Police Officer Nicholas O’Malley pleads not guilty to manslaughter in Roxbury shooting of Stephenson King Jr.
Prosecutors argue O'Malley's use of deadly force was excessive; defense claims he had no choice during the incident.
O'Malley, released on personal recognizance, must return to court on July 23, surrender firearms, and avoid contact with witnesses.

title: Embrace Boston to open a new civic space in Downtown Crossing
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Embrace Boston will transform a two-building complex at 33-41 West St. into a new civic center in Downtown Crossing.
The center will include rotating art exhibits, a podcast studio, and flexible programming space, catering to community gatherings.
With support from donors and local institutions, the center aims to open by Boston's 400th anniversary in 2030.

title: Man allegedly points gun during road rage incident in Bellingham
author: John Waller
bullets: Stephen Fahey, 61, of Franklin is charged with assault and firearm offenses after a road rage incident in Bellingham.
Fahey allegedly pointed a gun at another male on Maple Street and was later found in a 99 Restaurant parking lot.
He faces charges of assault by means of a dangerous weapon, carrying a firearm while intoxicated, and disturbing the peace.

title: A 21-year-old accessed a lake limited to Hopkinton residents. A 70-year-old allegedly confronted him and is now charged with attempted murder.
author: John Waller
bullets: 21-year-old man on crutches accessed Hopkinton-limited Lake Maspenock, prompting a violent confrontation.
70-year-old Steven Dana charged with attempted murder for holding the man underwater at Sandy Beach.
Dana also faces strangulation, suffocation, and assault charges; expected in Framingham District Court Thursday.
